metricComparisons.description: raising saturate should materially increase average color saturation

expectedVideo: higher saturate should visibly increase color intensity without posterizing the frame
expectedAudio: higher saturate should add audible harmonic richness (soft-clip warmth, even+odd harmonics) and raise perceived loudness without exceeding 0 dBFS true-peak
amount=0 should mute (matches grayscale on video); amount=1 should sound clean (identity-ish); amount>1 should ramp into saturation progressively
manualChecks: Does the harmonic saturation feel related to the color intensity increase (vivid colour ↔ warmer/richer tone)?
Is the saturation timbral (extra harmonics, tube-like) rather than dynamic (compression)? — contrast covers dynamics, saturate covers timbre.
Does the high-saturation setting remain musical rather than fuzzy/broken?
